title: rubus × densipiliformis
intro: Rubus × densipiliformis, a hybrid species of the Rubus genus, is a cross between the blackthorn (Rubus fruticosus) and the Himalayan blackberry (Rubus procerus), characterized by its dense, thorny stems and small white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus × densipiliformis include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support digestive health. Rubus × densipiliformis can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions and wounds, or internally to support overall health.
